purpose: Compact project-local target and submodel behavior evidence used by sequencing agents as advisory learning for effect suitability, readability, and safe probe planning.
planningRules: Treat display/target-behavior.json as project-local advisory evidence, not central/shared ground truth.
Key behavior learning by target or submodel fingerprint plus target kind, effect name/family, and probe scope.
Use records to choose safer targets, submodels, probes, and effect settings, but keep review/apply validation authoritative.
Preserve submodel records as first-class evidence for every parent model type.
Carry enriched parent and submodel context from display/model-index.json into behavior records.
Consolidate richer records under the same semantic aggregate instead of creating duplicates when metadata improves.
Do not infer user-specific semantic prop meaning from hard-coded target names.
notes: This contract describes curated portable training summaries and the project-local artifact shape. It is not a raw render observation export.
Central/shared training packages may consume anonymized summaries from this artifact, but user installations should retain their own project-local behavior evidence.
Custom models need this local learning because their structures are user-specific, but the same target/submodel framework applies to built-in models, groups, and custom models.
